jokes.text: Hey, Murray. Nice shorts.
jokes.setup: Casual greeting to their manager
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific compliment about Murray's shorts delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Classic deadpan FOTC opener - perfectly economical and character-specific

jokes.text: It should be called the 'boring buildings tour.'
jokes.setup: Murray enthusiastically introduces the 'interesting buildings tour'
jokes.punchline: Jemaine immediately deflates it with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jemaine's blunt honesty contrasting Murray's forced enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: It should be called the 'boring buildings tour.'

jokes.text: Why? - Because it's boring.
jokes.setup: Murray asks why it should be called boring
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's circular logic delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - maximum deadpan with minimum words

jokes.text: There's a real buzz about it. Everyone's talking about these tours.
jokes.setup: Murray defending his clearly unpopular building tours
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence about non-existent buzz
jokes.explanation: Pure Murray character comedy - his delusional manager optimism

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: There's a real buzz about it. Everyone's talking about these tours.

jokes.text: Bret, you were talking about it last week. - I was saying it's boring. - Oh, were you? Well, you were still talking about it.
jokes.setup: Murray tries to prove people are talking about his tours
jokes.punchline: His logic that complaining still counts as 'talking about it'
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic from Murray - technically correct but completely missing the point

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Bret, you were talking about it last week.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Oh, were you? Well, you were still talking about it.

jokes.text: You knew about that. I was gonna tell you the difference between, you know, gazebos and band shells.
jokes.setup: Murray defending his elaborate tour plans
jokes.punchline: His sincere excitement about incredibly mundane architectural distinctions
jokes.explanation: Murray's earnest passion for the most boring possible subject matter

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You knew about that. I was gonna tell you the difference between, you know, gazebos and band shells.

jokes.text: Friday, 4:05 P.M: Bret, Jemaine and Murray leave Dave's shop.
jokes.setup: Murray mentions he has a schedule
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously detailed itinerary for simple activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray character moment - over-organizing the mundane with corporate precision

dialogue.line: Friday, 4:05 P.M: Bret, Jemaine and Murray leave Dave's shop.

jokes.text: Where'd you meet her? - At... at the library. - Library? What library? - Video library.
jokes.setup: Jemaine claims to have a date
jokes.punchline: His obvious lie gets worse when pressed for details
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of Jemaine's awkward lying under pressure

jokes.text: I won't say goodbye, I don't feel like it. See you, Murray.
jokes.setup: Murray declares he won't say goodbye
jokes.punchline: Immediately says goodbye anyway
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contradiction showing Murray can't even commit to being petty

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I won't say goodbye, I don't feel like it.

jokes.text: Bye, Murray. - Bye. You gonna say 'bye'? - No!
jokes.setup: Murray's earlier declaration not to say goodbye
jokes.punchline: He breaks down and asks for acknowledgment, gets rejected
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Murray's failed petulance - shows he can't maintain dignity

jokes.text: The carrots were really nice. And the broccoli was really nice as well. Yeah. It did turn out really nice. Yeah, really nice. It was really nice. Yeah, it was really nice.
jokes.setup: Post-dinner conversation between new couple
jokes.punchline: Painfully repetitive small talk about vegetables
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - captures the painful reality of awkward first date conversation

dialogue.line: The carrots were really nice. And the broccoli was really nice as well.
It did turn out really nice.

jokes.text: Like your eyes... I could look at them for ages. The eggs were really nice like your lips. So... your lips look delicious like... as delicious as the eggs. Probably more delicious.
jokes.setup: Bret attempting romantic compliments
jokes.punchline: Comparing girlfriend's lips to breakfast eggs in increasingly awkward way
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Bret's earnest but terrible romantic comparison escalates beautifully

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Like your eyes... I could look at them for ages. The eggs were really nice like your lips. So... your lips look delicious like... as delicious as the eggs. Probably more delicious.

jokes.text: I don't know... why don't I stay and you go?
jokes.setup: Awkward three-way conversation about staying over
jokes.punchline: Jemaine suggests he stay with Coco while Bret leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist logic from Jemaine - completely inappropriate suggestion delivered casually

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I don't know... why don't I stay and you go?

jokes.text: It's good, it's good.
jokes.setup: Bret asks for feedback on his two-hour love song
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's immediate, unenthusiastic approval before hearing it
jokes.explanation: Classic Jemaine disinterest - dismissive approval without listening

jokes.text: It's a bit long. - Okay. - It's two hours long.
jokes.setup: Jemaine's understated criticism of the song length
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd actual length - two hours
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - 'a bit long' becomes hilariously inadequate description

jokes.text: Would you actually do that? - Probably not.
jokes.setup: Jemaine questions if Bret would really climb the highest mountain
jokes.punchline: Bret's immediate honest admission he wouldn't
jokes.explanation: Perfect deconstruction of romantic song cliches with Bret's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Well, you're sort of promising that you would. - That's a metaphor. - Oh, is it? Oh okay. - Yeah. - What's it a metaphor for? - For... that I'd do anything for her. - Would you climb the highest mountain for her?
jokes.setup: Bret explains his song is metaphorical
jokes.punchline: Jemaine takes the metaphor literally and circles back to the original question
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic from Jemaine - perfectly literal interpretation defeats metaphor explanation

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Well, you're sort of promising that you would.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: For... that I'd do anything for her.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Would you climb the highest mountain for her?

jokes.text: I would hang out with you. - Oh, that's good. That's really... - That's not bad. That's a lyric of mine. You can use that.
jokes.setup: Jemaine suggests more realistic song lyrics
jokes.punchline: His pride in the mundane 'hang out' lyric and generous offer to share it
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Jemaine's artistic generosity with his mundane 'creation'

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I would hang out with you.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: That's a lyric of mine. You can use that.

jokes.text: She has a rash that she doesn't tell anyone about.
jokes.setup: Jemaine asks for specific details about Coco for the song
jokes.punchline: Bret immediately shares inappropriate private medical information
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - Bret's complete lack of boundaries about girlfriend's privacy

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: She has a rash that she doesn't tell anyone about.

jokes.text: President. - Present. - Secretary. - Present. - Treasurer. - Present! - Maybe we should make that one title? - Sure. It's all... it's quite convoluted. You're all three.
jokes.setup: Official fan club meeting roll call
jokes.punchline: Mel is literally the entire membership filling all positions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - formal meeting structure with one-person membership

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Maybe we should make that one title?

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Sure. It's all... it's quite convoluted. You're all three.

jokes.text: I took some of Bret... well, actually I found them, I... when I was round at his flat. - Oh-ho! - That... he's asleep there
jokes.setup: Mel offers photos for the newsletter
jokes.punchline: Reveals she took/found creepy photos of sleeping Bret
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about obsessive fan behavior - crossing privacy boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: I took some of Bret... well, actually I found them, I... when I was round at his flat.

jokes.text: What's this one? - Mmm? - What's this one? - God, how did that get in there? Sorry. It must have slipped in with the... do you want to hold onto that? - No.
jokes.setup: Murray looking through Mel's photos
jokes.punchline: Mel has accidentally included inappropriate photo, offers to let Murray keep it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mel's inappropriate fan behavior with visual component

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: God, how did that get in there? Sorry. It must have slipped in with the... do you want to hold onto that?

jokes.text: That's really great. - You don't think Bret's any less available now that he's? - Well, no! No. Is she pretty?
jokes.setup: Mel claims she's happy about Bret's relationship
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals jealousy by asking if girlfriend is pretty
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mel's fake acceptance immediately undercut by jealousy

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You don't think Bret's any less available now that he's?

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Well, no! No. Is she pretty?

jokes.text: He's not gonna... not gonna get a girlfriend anytime soon, so... - Does, um... - Try your chances with him, can't you?
jokes.setup: Murray trying to console Mel about Bret having girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Suggests she pursue Jemaine as consolation prize
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Murray's awkward attempt at matchmaking with cruel honesty about Jemaine

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: He's not gonna... not gonna get a girlfriend anytime soon, so...

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Try your chances with him, can't you?

jokes.text: Does... does Bret's girlfriend look anything like me? - Little bit? - A little bit... around the eyes. - Oh yeah? Big eyes, huh? - Well, she's... she's got eyes.
jokes.setup: Mel fishing for validation about resembling Bret's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Murray's awkward deflection ending with stating the obvious
jokes.explanation: Excellent awkward comedy - Murray's desperate attempt to avoid hurting feelings

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: Does... does Bret's girlfriend look anything like me?

dialogue.speaker: Mel
dialogue.line: A little bit... around the eyes.

jokes.text: If You're Into It [entire song]
jokes.setup: Bret performs romantic song for Coco
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate sexual suggestions involving roommate Stuart
jokes.explanation: Masterful musical comedy - starts innocent but escalates to hilariously inappropriate threesome suggestions

jokes.text: Kiss her, Bret. Kiss her.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Bret and Coco
jokes.punchline: Jemaine coaching from the sidelines like a sports commentator
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Jemaine treating intimate moment like a performance to optimize

jokes.text: She doesn't like... she doesn't want to go to a strip bar again.
jokes.setup: Jemaine suggesting strip bar for date
jokes.punchline: Implies they've already been to strip bars together
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection revealing their unconventional date history

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: She doesn't like... she doesn't want to go to a strip bar again.

jokes.text: Or she doesn't always have to come as well. That's another option. Me and you could go on one of these date... oh, that'd be weird.
jokes.setup: Jemaine suggesting alternatives to three-person dates
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-sentence that him and Bret dating would be weird
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-awareness moment - Jemaine's logic leads to uncomfortable realization

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Or she doesn't always have to come as well. That's another option. Me and you could go on one of these date... oh, that'd be weird.

jokes.text: Coco said that the other night when I went to the bathroom that she felt like you... like made a move on her. - Yep. Yep, guilty, I did.
jokes.setup: Bret confronts Jemaine about inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's immediate casual admission of guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jemaine character moment - no shame or denial, just matter-of-fact admission

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Coco said that the other night when I went to the bathroom that she felt like you... like made a move on her.

jokes.text: But you weren't doing anything, so I thought...
jokes.setup: Jemaine explaining why he made a move on Bret's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His logic that Bret's inaction gave him permission
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - Jemaine's complete misunderstanding of relationship boundaries

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: But you weren't doing anything, so I thought...

jokes.text: You were going on their dates? - I know. Yeah. You don't go on other people's dates. - I thought you had your own date. - No, I was dating them.
jokes.setup: Murray discovers Jemaine has been third-wheeling
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's matter-of-fact explanation that he was dating both of them
jokes.explanation: Perfect character revelation - Jemaine's complete obliviousness to social norms

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You were going on their dates?

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: You don't go on other people's dates.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I thought you had your own date.

jokes.text: Are your feelings hurt? - No. - They must be. Are they? - No. - A little bit? - No. - A little bit, eh? - A little bit.
jokes.setup: Murray trying to get Jemaine to admit his feelings are hurt
jokes.punchline: Gradual breakdown of Jemaine's denial through persistent questioning
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure showing Jemaine's emotional walls crumbling

jokes.text: I just hope we don't have a Yoko Ono situation. - Do you know what I mean? - Yeah. Is she trying to split up the band?
jokes.setup: Murray referencing the classic band-breaking-up trope
jokes.punchline: Direct comparison to Beatles/Yoko situation with their tiny New Zealand band
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor - applying famous band dynamics to their struggling duo

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: I just hope we don't have a Yoko Ono situation.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Do you know what I mean?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah. Is she trying to split up the band?

jokes.text: Black-haired lady queen / Dabbling in art / I won't let the she-wolf / Tear us apart
jokes.setup: Jemaine writing an anti-Coco song
jokes.punchline: The over-the-top metaphors and hostile imagery for an innocent girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jemaine's jealousy manifesting as dramatic songwriting.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Black-haired lady queen / Dabbling in art / I won't let the she-wolf / Tear us apart

jokes.text: Like this pencil... look. [breaks pencil] Jeez, that is a... That's it... that's your band. Broken.
jokes.setup: Murray trying to explain relationship dangers to the band
jokes.punchline: Melodramatic pencil-breaking metaphor demonstration
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor comedy - Murray's overwrought demonstration of band dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: Jeez, that is a... That's it... that's your band. Broken.

jokes.text: It's a rare thing, what you've got there. - Hi, I'm not disturbing you guys, am I? - Hey, Coco. - No, come in. - We're busy at the moment actually.
jokes.setup: Murray just praising Bret's relationship as Coco arrives
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction as Jemaine rudely dismisses her
jokes.explanation: Good timing and ironic structure. Shows the immediate contradiction between theory and practice.

dialogue.speaker: Murray
dialogue.line: It's a rare thing, what you've got there.

dialogue.speaker: Coco
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm not disturbing you guys, am I?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: We're busy at the moment actually.

jokes.text: I had some spare time last night, and... [shows poster] - Whoa, that's awesome. - Well, it's really arty.
jokes.setup: Coco presents her artistic poster for the band
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's backhanded compliment calling it 'arty'
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment - Jemaine's subtle dismissal of Coco's contribution

dialogue.speaker: Coco
dialogue.line: I had some spare time last night, and...

jokes.text: And also present, Coco. Yoko. What did you... what did you say? Oh nothing, just had a bit of a cough. Mmm, okay. No, it wasn't a real cough. I said 'Yoko.'
jokes.setup: Jemaine 'accidentally' calls Coco 'Yoko'
jokes.punchline: Escalates by admitting it wasn't a cough and repeating the insult
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Jemaine making the conflict explicit rather than backing down

dialogue.speaker: Coco
dialogue.line: What did you... what did you say?

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh nothing, just had a bit of a cough.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: No, it wasn't a real cough. I said 'Yoko.'

jokes.text: Oh-no, she isn't. Jemaine, you're putting 'oh no' together to sound like 'Ono.' - Oh-no, I didn't. - You did it again.
jokes.setup: Jemaine denying the Yoko comparison
jokes.punchline: Unconsciously continues making Yoko puns while denying it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay escalation - Jemaine trapped by his own subconscious puns

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Oh-no, she isn't. Jemaine, you're putting 'oh no' together to sound like 'Ono.'

jokes.text: Well, Yoko's trying to split up the band.
jokes.setup: Murray asks what the problem is with Coco
jokes.punchline: Jemaine doubles down on the Yoko comparison explicitly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Jemaine abandoning pretense and stating his belief directly

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Well, Yoko's trying to split up the band.

jokes.text: Don't be such a... a dickhead. - Yeah well, I'm quitting this band. - Yeah. - Well, you quit last week. - Yeah well, I'm quitting again.
jokes.setup: Band argument escalating to quitting
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jemaine quits regularly, so he's quitting again
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing this is a pattern, deflating the drama of quitting

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Don't be such a... a dickhead.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Yeah well, I'm quitting this band.

jokes.text: Sorry, everyone. It was a visa application denied. It took a long time to try and get resolved. I think the guy's a criminal. Sorry. Just carry on. It's looking good.
jokes.setup: Meeting has devolved into chaos and fighting
jokes.punchline: Bureaucrat entering with unrelated visa information, calling someone a criminal, then cheerfully saying 'it's looking good'
jokes.explanation: Surreal interruption that contrasts beautifully with the band chaos. The oblivious optimism is well-timed.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown government worker
dialogue.line: Sorry, everyone. It was a visa application denied. It took a long time to try and get resolved. I think the guy's a criminal. Sorry. Just carry on. It's looking good.

jokes.text: Do you miss Bret? A little bit. Do you? Same. I miss him. Can you ask him back in the band? Okay.
jokes.setup: Murray and Jemaine alone after the band breakup
jokes.punchline: Their immediate admission they miss Bret and quick decision to ask him back
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing their genuine friendship beneath the conflict. Very economical resolution.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Can you ask him back in the band?

jokes.text: Dear Jemaine, by the time you read this, I will be far far away at Coco's house
jokes.setup: Dramatic letter opening suggesting Bret has fled far away
jokes.punchline: The revelation he's just at his girlfriend's house
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of dramatic departure letter tropes. The bathos is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: Dear Jemaine, by the time you read this, I will be far far away at Coco's house

jokes.text: Lives are like retractable pencils / If you push 'em too hard, they're gonna break / And people are like paper dolls
jokes.setup: Philosophical song about relationships
jokes.punchline: Absurd metaphor comparing people to paper dolls and retractable pencils
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to pencil metaphor expanded into full philosophical song

dialogue.line: Lives are like retractable pencils / If you push 'em too hard, they're gonna break / And people are like paper dolls

jokes.text: You search on the roll / You search on the roll / You're searchin' round the roll / You search with your fingernail / Again and again and again and again / And again
jokes.setup: Song about love being like tape
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of the frustrating experience of finding tape end
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy in musical form - universal frustrating experience

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: You search on the roll / You search on the roll / You're searchin' round the roll / You search with your fingernail / Again and again and again and again / And again

jokes.text: Are you sure? - Yeah. - Oh, I thought you... I was sure you were gonna say the band.
jokes.setup: Jemaine gives Bret ultimatum between girl and band
jokes.punchline: His genuine surprise that Bret chooses girlfriend over band
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jemaine's genuine shock at normal human priorities

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought you... I was sure you were gonna say the band.

jokes.text: I guess you can still be in the band.
jokes.setup: After dramatic ultimatum and band breakup
jokes.punchline: Jemaine casually reverses position with minimal fanfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - all the drama resolved with casual acceptance

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: I guess you can still be in the band.

jokes.text: Actually, I'm gonna wait till the bus stops, and then I'll get off.
jokes.setup: After emotional reconciliation on moving bus
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's practical consideration about bus safety
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jemaine's practicality undercutting emotional moment

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Actually, I'm gonna wait till the bus stops, and then I'll get off.

jokes.text: I've been thinking about love, and I guess it's the very strongest adhesive. Oh, sorry, Bret, were you talking to me? I was humming.
jokes.setup: Bret offers philosophical insight about love
jokes.punchline: Jemaine wasn't listening, was absorbed in his own song
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending character moment - Jemaine's self-absorption preventing genuine connection

dialogue.speaker: Bret
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about love, and I guess it's the very strongest adhesive.

dialogue.speaker: Jemaine
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry, Bret, were you talking to me? I was humming.
